title: Safe Web Browsing
subtitle: Stay Smart, Stay Safe Online!
overview: This session helps students learn how to browse the internet safely, protect personal information, identify fake websites, avoid cyber threats, and become responsible digital citizens.

images._note: Add photos from cyber safety activities, safe browsing demos, students discussing phishing examples, and classroom presentations.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Poster showing safe web browsing rules
gallery.caption: Students learning the rules of safe internet browsing
Strong passwords help protect online accounts

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Browser showing HTTPS secure lock icon
gallery.caption: Always check for HTTPS and the lock symbol
Practicing safe browsing in the computer lab

keyTopics: Importance of keeping personal information private
Strong Passwords — Mix letters, numbers, and symbols
Phishing Scams — Fake emails and messages
Avoiding Unsafe Downloads and Unknown Links
Digital Footprint — What you post stays online
Using Antivirus and Safe Search Tools

youtubeVideos.title: How to Create Strong Passwords
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how strong passwords protect your accounts from hackers.

activity.title: Spot the Safe Website Activity
steps: Check whether the URL starts with HTTPS
Look for the lock icon in the browser
Identify fake popups or suspicious ads
Discuss which websites are safe or unsafe
Create a list of internet safety rules

quiz.q: What should you check before entering personal information on a website?
quiz.explanation: HTTPS and the lock symbol show that the website connection is secure.

quiz.q: Which of these is a strong password?
quiz.explanation: Strong passwords use letters, numbers, and symbols together.

options: A fake attempt to steal information online
quiz.explanation: Phishing scams trick users into giving personal information.

quiz.q: What should you do if a stranger messages you online?
options: Ignore and tell a trusted adult
quiz.explanation: Never share personal information with strangers online.

quiz.q: Which symbol usually means a website is secure?
quiz.explanation: A lock symbol indicates a secure website connection.

quiz.q: What should you do before downloading a file?
options: Check if the source is trusted
quiz.explanation: Only download files from trusted websites.
Cyberbullying means hurting or harassing others online.

quiz.q: Why should you avoid clicking unknown links?
options: They may contain viruses or scams
quiz.explanation: Unknown links can lead to unsafe websites or malware.
Everything you post online becomes part of your digital footprint.

quiz.q: Who should you tell if something online makes you uncomfortable?
quiz.explanation: Always tell a trusted adult if you face problems online.

quiz.q: Which is safe to share online?
quiz.explanation: Personal and financial information should never be shared online.
Antivirus software helps detect and remove harmful programs.

quiz.q: What should you do after using a public computer?
quiz.explanation: Always log out from accounts on shared or public computers.
HTTPS websites are generally safer and encrypted.

quiz.q: Why is safe web browsing important?
options: To protect personal information and devices
quiz.explanation: Safe browsing protects users from scams, viruses, and online dangers.
